ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Nɛ lɔkɔ a na kamɛ a, Yesu to Galilea ɛya nɛ Yohanes sɛkɛ̃ nɛ Yordan ɔwɔ a abuĩ kĩ ɛwɔla ye mui.
translated: Now when Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew to Galilee.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Lɛmɛ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yaalɛ fubusuotsa nɛ Hũhũ Kpalɛwa a akũ ni, Baguma lɛláakɔa ete ye da. Kitonɛkĩ ɛkɛna tɔkpa kĩ taawa daa.”
translated: The man and his wife and his children were sitting in the house of Hũhũ Kpalɛwa and his wife, Baguma was lying down and she slept. The door was closed with a big stick.

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: ɛkɔa ɛnado nɛ ye mɔawɛ linikɛ vɔɛ̃ kĩ ɛfɔ̃ bɛnyɛ nɛ de kpale ɔkɛ kamɛ a. Emininya de kpale esua linikɛ a onukpɛ̃ ade ɛdzakũ a.
translated: The farmer went to the farm and saw that the crops were ripe for harvesting. He then called the villagers to come and help him harvest the crops.

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Kĩ ɛdã bati a kĩ bidzi bisinya ye ɛnake a, ɛlɛ kĩ, “Midã, ade mma mɔ nwaɛ̃ amɛ nɛ awĩ a!
translated: The child took a stick and hit the dog's head and the dog's owner said, "My friend, your child has killed my dog!"

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: kitonɛkĩ Otidziwa Obi a ya kĩ yaatɛ bikĩ bɛyɛla a bɛna nkpã.
translated: The chief of Obi's town has gone to the farm with his children to work.

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Be kamɛ odima tápuli ɛkpa Yesu mbuayɛ, ade kito luwi a kanaa a, odima tádo ɔwɔlɛ ebuki evia ye kalɛ kadima.
translated: When the people heard Jesus' words, they were amazed at his teaching, because he taught them as one who had authority, not as their teachers of the law.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Awã naa, Yesu bi ye kĩ ɛdzakũ mɔatsɛ ɛnaa, lɛmɛ esi ye mbla mɔ ɔsĩ mɔ ɔsĩ kĩ,
translated: The woman said, Jesus looked at her with love in his eyes, and her heart was calm and all her troubles were gone.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Foesũ Yesu dĩ gba nɛ Galilea tite akũ flee. Yesu Tsa Bati Beblebee Fɔsɔ.
translated: Jesus went to Galilee to the other side of the lake. Jesus saw two brothers, Simon who was called Peter and Andrew.

ref: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
text: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Ɔnɔkɔɛ̃, fɔ̃ boe anɛ ebinya.”
translated: The tortoise said to the dog, "Friend, I want to go and ease myself."

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Mitawɛ akũ kana fɔtsa miyi akũ nɛ tite kĩĩ akũ, awã kĩ fupuka mɔ nkandɔlɔ aabula foe kade, ade bayekpanɛ lɛmɛ abubuli kɔla kawo kaye foe a.
translated: The hunter took his cutlass and his dog and went into the bush, but when he got to where the antelopes were, he saw that they had all gone, and he was left with nothing but the bones of the one that the lion had eaten.

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: foesũ bɛwɛ kĩ bapi ye. Lɛmɛ bivila bati kɔdabu a, kitonɛkĩ bati a bu Yesu ndɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ aye.
translated: They went to the farm and worked. In the evening when they returned home, the elder told them about Jesus and God who loves us very much.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Onyole odi nɛ Yudafɔ katsɛnakɔ̃ banɔkɔɛ̃ a kamɛ ɔmɔa kĩ bavɛ Yairo ya nɛ awã. Kĩ ɛmɔ Yesu a, ɛkɛna akũ ɛkpa ye.
translated: They came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gerasenes. And when Jesus had stepped out of the boat, immediately a man out of the tombs with an unclean spirit met him.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Ade Yesu bi be kĩ, “Mikɔa foe mima yĩ nɛ awĩ.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "My food is to do the will of Him who sent me."

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Oti a via ye kĩ, “Tɛbla tɛwɔdabe?” Yesu kpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Takɔ oti, tabɔa adɔpaa, taye, tadi adansiɛ klumu,
translated: He asked them, "Do you have anything to eat?" Jesus said to them, "Bring some of the fish you just caught."

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ke liti a, ɛnyɛ be osi kĩ bɛnaa Betlehem mɔ ɔlɛdo kĩ, “Minaawɛ obisɛ̃ a kawɛ wĩ. Ntɛ mimɔ́ ye ni, mifɔ̃ ĩnyi lɔkɔa yĩ malo nnaasum ye.”
translated: The angel said to them, "Do not be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be for all the people."

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Yesu kpa be mbuayɛ kĩ, “Mitákã nɛ Katsɛlɛ Kpalɛwa a kamɛ kĩ nɛ kayɔkayi a, otsole mɔ onyole Ɔbɔanɛ bɔa?
translated: Jesus said to them, "If I by the finger of God cast out demons, then the Kingdom of God has come upon you."

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: Kĩ míanyi kĩ Otidziwa Obi a deĩ kɔbɛ̃ nɛ katinya kĩĩ kamɛ kĩ yakɔa bati titikpa kate sũ ni,” Yesu bi oti a kĩ eveliveli a kĩ,
translated: The woman said to Jesus, "Sir, I can see that you are a prophet, our ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews claim that the place where we must worship is in Jerusalem."

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Foesũ mikɛna lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa kĩ bibi mi kĩ mikɛna a mipi foe midomɔ akũ. Lɛmɛ mitadã be fɔkɛlatsa mikɛna, kitonɛkĩ baákɛna nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ balɛ a akũ.
translated: The farmer's wife went to the farm and the farmer also went to the farm with the wife to harvest corn. The wife's basket was full of corn, the husband's was also full and the farm was full and the house was also full.

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Bɛkpa ye mbuayɛ kĩ, “Nɛ ɔnɔkɔali kamɛ ni, yaawɔla bati kpa a na, lɔkɔa ɛkɔa kɔba a edo nɛ kɔba akũ badãmɛnanɛ bɛbã awɛ, bikĩ baakɔa ye kanyakɔ̃ bɛma ye nɛ fudutsa kakpɔta lɔkɔ kamɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ.”
translated: They said to the rabbit, "When the farmer comes, you will carry the basket and I will go to the farm and dig up all the yams and bring them, and when I bring them, we will share them equally, and we will eat until we are satisfied, and then we will go to the forest and sleep."

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Ye ɔlate a aablɔmɛna ye, ɛvɛlɛ ye sɔtɔ ɛfɔ̃ ɛna tsɛfã mɔ anɛ kamɛ kɛkɛ fɔtsa bakɛnanɛ. Ade awã yaabe ɛba adɔ̃ a.
translated: They brought the matter to him, and he sat quietly for a while, then he said let us go to the elder's house. The elder said come and sit down.

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Ade awã mɔ maabi be kĩ, ‘Ĩnyína mi koũ. Midzakũ nɛ yĩ anɛ kamɛ, mi tɔkpa bakɛnanɛ!’ Yesu Fɔtsa Katsa Nɛ Fɔla Bakenɛ Nviã Akũ.
translated: The woman said to the people, 'My daughter is dead. Come and put your hands on her so that she will be healed and live!' Jesus Christ went and took the girl by the hand.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Fukĩĩ flee la ndɛ ɔmatsɔ̃ sɛmɛ kapi kawo a kayɔkayi aye.
translated: The woman went to the farm to work but the rain drove her back home.

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Adeke Yesu si ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a mbla kĩ bɛtabi odima kĩ ye nyɛ Kristo a. Yesu Lɛ Kɔdzɛla Nɛ Ye Lubokamɔ Mɔ Wuda Akũ.
translated: Jesus Christ is the foundation of the church and the head of the church is Christ. Jesus is the savior of the world and our Lord forever.

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “Nɛ lɔkɔ gɛlɛɛlɛ liti a, be ɔlate a muniki ɛya, ade ɛkɛna akunta mɔ be a.
translated: The man is very strong, he can uproot that tree, but he cannot count money.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ade awã bɛkpa ye waĩ kĩ bɛtsɛka mɔ otsonu mɔ ɔnyɛ kĩ ɛmɔa a, lɛmɛ kĩ ɛta be onukpɛ̃ ɛdã a, esĩ be kamɔa.
translated: The hunter shot an animal in the forest and carried it on his head to the village, and when he got home, his wife cooked it, and they ate together.

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Kĩ ɛkalɛ kɔdzɛla a, luflututu fitaa ka lɛtsa be, ade odo nɛ́ eto de kamɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ade yĩ Obi kĩ maátunɔ ye kalɛ a, yawo yĩ anɛ. Mitse ye!”
translated: The chief summoned him, the horn blower blew the horn and the people gathered, then he said to the crowd, "Obi's son has come to our village, let us go and see him!"

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Nɔ lɛgãkanya lɛya. Bɛkɛna nɔ kawɛ nɛ tite akũ, ndɛ ayekĩ bakɛna nɛ ode a.
translated: The man is not a farmer. He has gone to the forest to cut sticks, but his wife is at home.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Yesu via ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a kĩ, “Nde sũ libe wo mi lɔmɔ? Mitátɛ yĩ mido?”
translated: Jesus looked at them with anger and said, "Why do you trouble my mind? What is my fault?"

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Foesũ kpa mbla kĩ bɛdzɔlɛ ye kuni a akũ kadzɔlɛ wĩ kɛnaawo luwi lɛlalɛde a akũ. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye ni, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a aapuli bɛnaaye ɔnɔkpa a lɔkɔa bibi bati kĩ bɛdzɛla ye bitomɛna banɔkpa kamɛ. Ntɛ fɔya lɔmɔ ni, liti fɔvã a aaba fɔtɔnyade a.”
translated: The elder said that the reason they don't allow women to climb the mountain is that a long time ago, a woman went to the mountain and was possessed by the spirits and she came back with madness. When she returned home, she started speaking in a strange language and was saying things that didn't make sense. From that day on, they made a law that women should not climb the mountain again.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: ndɛ Otidziwa Obi a kĩ ɛtáya kĩ bisum ye, mbom kĩ yaasum ɛkɔa ye nkpã ɛkpa ɛnyɛ bati pii.” Yesu Tsa Banɛkunɛ Nviã.
translated: And the prophet Obi said to them, "You are not worthy to have me as your king, but I will give you a sign that you will not be able to deny." Jesus is the King of Kings.
